ttl.name: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM (for ADVICS Made)

para.dtcname: Lost Communication with Yaw Rate Sensor Module
Lost Communication with Lateral Acceleration Sensor Module
Lost Communication with Steering Angle Sensor Module

content5.ptxt: The skid control ECU receives the signals from the ECM, steering angle sensor, and yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system.

entry.ptxt: When any of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, after the output of data from the skid control ECU is completed, the sending continues for 5 seconds or more.
With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, the condition that bus OFF state occurs once or more within 100 ms occurs 10 times in succession. (Sent signals cannot be received.)
With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, a delay in receiving data from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and steering angle sensor continues for 1 second or more.

With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, the condition that a delay in receiving data from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and steering angle sensor occurs more than once within 5 seconds occurs 10 times in succession within 60 seconds.

entry.ptxt: When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more and the vehicle speed 9 mph (15 km/h) or more, data cannot be sent to the ECM for 2 seconds or more.
The IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more and the vehicle speed 9 mph (15 km/h) or more for 2 seconds or more.

entry.ptxt: When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, data from the yaw rate sensor cannot be received for 1 second or more.
With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, the following occurs 10 times in succession within 60 seconds.
The condition that data from the yaw rate sensor cannot be received occurs once or more within 5 seconds.

entry.ptxt: (Skid control ECU to yaw rate and acceleration sensor)
When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, data from the acceleration sensor cannot be received for 1 second or more.
With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, the following occurs 10 times in succession within 60 seconds.
The condition that data from the acceleration sensor cannot be received occurs once or more within 5 seconds.

entry.ptxt: (Skid control ECU to yaw rate and acceleration sensor)
When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, data from the steering angle sensor cannot be received for 1 second or more.
With the IG1 terminal voltage 10 V or more, the following occurs 10 times in succession within 60 seconds.
The condition that data from the steering angle sensor cannot be received occurs once or more within 5 seconds.

entry.ptxt: (Skid control ECU to steering angle sensor)

test1.ptxt: Using the intelligent tester, check for any momentary interruption in the wire harness and connector corresponding to a DTC (See page ).

entry.ptxt: EFI communication open detection / ERROR or NORMAL
Steering sensor open detection / ERROR or NORMAL
Yaw rate sensor open detection / ERROR or NORMAL
There is a constant open circuit

atten4.ptxt: Perform the above inspection before removing the sensor and connector.

testgrp.testtitle: CHECK IF EACH SENSOR AND ECM CONNECTOR IS SECURELY CONNECTED

test1.ptxt: Check if each sensor or ECM connector is securely connected.

specitem.ptxt: The connector should be securely connected.

test1.ptxt: Record the output DTCs (for ABS, VSC, and CAN communication) (See page ).

atten4.ptxt: If the CAN communication system DTC and the relevant sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, troubleshoot the relevant sensor DTCs (for ABS and VSC) after the CAN communication system returns to normal.

entry.ptxt: DTC (CAN communication system DTC) is output
DTC (ABS and/or VSC DTC) is output

testgrp.testtitle: REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR

test1.ptxt: Repair or replace the harness or connector.
Check for any momentary interruption between the skid control ECU and each sensor or ECM (See page ).
Check that there is no momentary interruption.
Drive the vehicle and turn the steering wheel to the right and left at a speed of 9 mph (15 km/h) or more.
Check that no CAN communication system DTC is output.
If ABS and VSC DTCs are output, record them.

entry.ptxt: DTC output for the CAN communication system
No DTC is output (ABS and/or VSC DTC is output)
No DTC is output (No ABS and/or VSC DTC is output)

atten4.ptxt: The CAN communication system must be normal when repairing each sensor DTC (for ABS and VSC).

testgrp.testtitle: CONNECT CONNECTOR TO EACH SENSOR OR ECM CORRECTLY
REPAIR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTC
